vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
speaking in a clear, strong, and beautiful way
b
the level of formal or casual language you choose
c
the ability to speak smoothly, without stopping too much
d
the quality of being trusted and believed by others
e
confident and direct, in a calm and positive way
f
polite and professional, for serious situations
g
a friendly, easy connection between two people
h
short and clear, with no extra words
i
natural and not planned in advance
j
able to express your ideas clearly in words

reading.rtitle: Flavia Speaks at an International Event
paras: Today is a big day for Flavia. She is at a full international professional event, and she will present Sebrae to partners from many countries. She starts with a formal greeting: 'Good morning. It is a pleasure to be here.' Her register is professional, not casual. She speaks with fluency, and her calm voice gives her credibility.

Flavia is assertive. When a partner asks a question, she gives a concise answer, short and clear. She talks about geographic indications, the certifications that protect products from a special region. She is articulate about the mission to Italy, and her eloquent words help the partners understand the value of Brazilian products.

During the coffee break, Flavia builds rapport with the guests. Her tone is friendly, but still professional. When a partner asks a surprising question, she gives a spontaneous answer with confidence. At the end of the event, Flavia feels proud. After twenty lessons, she can speak with confidence at a real international event.
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A2 reading about speaking with confidence at an international professional event: register, fluency, and credibility

gapfill.text: At a professional event, choose your register. Use a formal greeting, speak with fluency, and give concise answers. Be assertive but polite. A good register builds your credibility and your rapport with the partners.

bank.label: Useful sentences to speak with confidence at an event
items: Good morning. It is a pleasure to be here.
I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP.
Let me give you a concise summary of our work.
Our geographic indications protect products from a special region.
I am happy to talk about our mission to Italy.
I would be happy to answer your questions.
Thank you for your time. It was a pleasure to meet you.

lf.title: Complete the story of the event

lf.text: Remember: at a professional event, use a formal register, speak with fluency, be concise, and build rapport. That is speaking with confidence.

items.situation: You open your presentation at an international event. Greet the audience professionally.
tips: Good morning. It is a pleasure to be here.

items.situation: A partner asks who you are. Introduce yourself with credibility.
tips: I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP.
I work with small businesses and international missions.

items.situation: A partner asks about geographic indications. Give a concise answer.
tips: Geographic indications protect products from a special region.
They give our products more value and credibility.

items.situation: The event is ending. Close with a professional goodbye.
tips: Thank you for your time. It was a pleasure to meet you.
I look forward to working with you.

items: Open the event: greet the audience with a formal register and introduce yourself. Use 'Good morning' and 'I am a consultant at Sebrae-SP.'
Present Sebrae and geographic indications in a concise, articulate way. Be assertive and clear, and talk about the mission to Italy.
Build rapport with a guest. Keep a friendly but professional tone, and give a spontaneous answer to a question.
Do the full event in three steps -- opening, presentation, and coffee break -- with no notes.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Opening: 'Good morning. It is a pleasure to be here. I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP.'
Presentation: 'Let me give you a concise summary. Our geographic indications protect products from a special region.'
Coffee break: 'It is a pleasure to meet you. I am happy to tell you more about our mission to Italy.'
Closing: 'Thank you for your time. I look forward to working with you.'
answerkey.note: A confident professional uses a formal register, speaks with fluency, and builds rapport with the partners.

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone. It is a pleasure to be here at this international event. I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP. Today, I will present our work with small businesses and geographic indications. These certifications protect products from a special region and give them more value. I am also happy to talk about our mission to Italy. Thank you for your time, and I would be happy to answer your questions.

Here is one simple tip to speak with confidence at a professional event. Think about your register. In a casual chat, you can say hi, how is it going. But at a formal event, say: good morning, it is a pleasure to be here. Keep your answers concise, short and clear. Be assertive but polite. And build rapport with a friendly, professional tone. A good register gives you credibility. Good luck at your next event!

extra_audio.text: First, Flavia opens the event with a formal greeting and introduces herself. Then, she presents Sebrae and the geographic indications in a concise way. Next, she talks about the mission to Italy with credibility. After that, she builds rapport with the guests during the coffee break. Finally, she closes the event and thanks the partners for their time.
